content.problemStatement:
**This is the easy version of the problem. The only difference between this and the hard version is that here, you're given the entire array AAA.**

Consider a tree on NNN vertices, with vertex iii having the value AiA_iAi​ associated with it.

The score of the tree with respect to array AAA, denoted f(A)f(A)f(A), is defined as follows:


For each edge (u,v)(u, v)(u,v) of the tree, give it the value Au+AvA_u + A_vAu​+Av​.
The score of the tree is then the greatest common divisor (GCD) of the values of all the edges.
You are given a tree on NNN vertices, and an array AAA of length NNN.

Compute the minimum value of f(A)f(A)f(A) across all rearrangements of this array AAA.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_{N}A1​,A2​,…,AN​.
The next N−1N-1N−1 lines describe the tree. The iii-th of them contains two space-separated integers uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​, denoting an edge between these two vertices.




Output Format
For each test case, output the answer on a new line: the minimum possible value of f(A)f(A)f(A) across all rearrangements of AAA.
constraints: The input edges describe a tree on the vertex set {1,2,…,N}\{1, 2, \ldots, N\}{1,2,…,N}.
The sum of NNN and the sum of max⁡(A)\max(A)max(A) across all tests both won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.
